name: ruby-levenshtein
title: ruby-levenshtein
summary: calculates the Levenshtein distance between two sequences
description:
calculates the Levenshtein distance between two sequences
The Levenshtein distance is a metric for measuring the amount of difference
between two sequences (i.e., the so called edit distance). The Levenshtein
distance between two sequences is given by the minimum number of operations
needed to transform one sequence into the other, where an operation is an
insertion, deletion, or substitution of a single element.
.
This Ruby library can work on strings, arrays or any other objects provided
the sequences respond to :each and the objects to :hash and :eql?.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-levenshtein because calculates the Levenshtein distance between two sequences.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-levenshtein as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-levenshtein with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-levenshtein --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
